page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 4 dedicated physical cores built on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of average and weak results. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests score in the average tier, as do large-block memory bandwidth reads of 71.58 GB/sec and writes of 34.94 GB/sec. However, single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores fall into the weak category, and Passmark CPU scores are rated as poor. Database operations perform at an average level, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are weaker. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware AES acceleration, though other advanced instruction sets like AVX are absent due to the ARM64 architecture.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and high memory density offer a favorable profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-frequency x86 processors. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed, low-latency local data access without the added cost of network-attached storage. This makes the instance suitable for memory-bound applications, database caching, and medium-scale database operations, though it is less optimal for compute-heavy tasks or LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance in database operations and large-block memory bandwidth, though CPU-heavy tasks and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. The combination of high memory capacity and local NVMe storage makes this instance a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database applications that benefit from local scratch space, while avoiding the overhead of x86 architectures.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores, high memory density, and local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Includes 237 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers average performance in database operations and large-block memory bandwidth
Best suited for memory-bound applications, database caching, and local-storage workloads
